intro: Maxillaria huancabambae, also known as the "huancabambae orchid," is a species of orchid native to the Andes mountain range in Peru. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Maxillaria huancabambae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat respiratory issues and as a natural expectorant.
